src.osir_service.osir_service.smb package

Submodules

src.osir_service.osir_service.smb.SMBService module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.smb.SMBService.SMBMounter(mount_point, username=None, password=None, version='3.0', check_interval=15)[source]

Bases: object

Manages the mounting, monitoring, and unmounting of an SMB share, ensuring continuous accessibility.

__init__(mount_point, username=None, password=None, version='3.0', check_interval=15)[source]

Initializes the SMBMounter with the necessary parameters for mounting an SMB share.

Parameters:

  • mount_point (str) – The local directory where the SMB share will be mounted.

  • username (Optional[str]) – The username for accessing the SMB share.

  • password (Optional[str]) – The password for accessing the SMB share.

  • version (str) – The SMB protocol version to use.

  • check_interval (int) – The interval in seconds between checks to ensure the mount is still active.

download_file(remote_relative_path: str, local_path: str) → bool[source]

Copies a file from the mounted SMB share to the local filesystem (Download).

Parameters:

  • remote_relative_path (str) – The path relative to the mount point of the file to download. Ex: ‘files/input.txt’

  • local_path (str) – The local destination path (can be a directory or a file path).

Returns:

True if the file was downloaded successfully, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

mount()[source]

Attempts to mount the SMB share at the specified mount point.

Returns:

True if the share is successfully mounted, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

start_monitoring()[source]

Starts a background thread that continuously monitors the mount point to ensure it remains mounted and accessible.

stop_monitoring()[source]

Stops the monitoring thread.

unmount()[source]

Attempts to unmount the SMB share.

Returns:

True if the share is successfully unmounted, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

upload_file(local_path: str, remote_relative_path: str) → bool[source]

Copies a file from the local filesystem to the mounted SMB share (Upload).

Parameters:

  • local_path (str) – The absolute or relative path of the file to upload locally.

  • remote_relative_path (str) – The path relative to the mount point where the file should be placed. Ex: ‘results/report.json’

Returns:

True if the file was uploaded successfully, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool
